description: List #Preview blocks in a Swift source file

filePath.description: Absolute path to a Swift source file

name: preview_list

description: Compile and launch a live SwiftUI preview. Returns a session ID. Supports macOS (default) and iOS simulator.

deviceUDID.description: Simulator device UDID (for ios; auto-selects if omitted)

filePath.description: Absolute path to a Swift source file containing #Preview

headless.description: If false, shows the preview window (default: true)

height.description: Window height in points (macOS only, default: 600)

projectPath.description: Project root path (auto-detected if omitted). Enables importing project types from SPM packages, Bazel swift_library targets, or Xcode projects (.xcodeproj / .xcworkspace).

scheme.description: Xcode scheme name (only used for .xcodeproj / .xcworkspace projects). Required when the project contains more than one scheme and none of them match the source file's directory.

width.description: Window width in points (macOS only, default: 400)

name: preview_start

description: Capture a screenshot of a running preview. Returns the image as JPEG (default) or PNG.

quality.description: JPEG quality 0.0–1.0 (default: 0.85). Values >= 1.0 produce PNG output.

name: preview_snapshot

description: Close a preview and clean up the session.
name: preview_stop

description: Change rendering traits (color scheme, dynamic type, locale, layout direction, legibility weight) for a running preview. Triggers recompile; @State is reset. Pass empty string to clear a trait. Note: dynamicTypeSize only has a visible effect on iOS simulator — macOS does not scale fonts in response to this modifier.

layoutDirection.description: Layout direction: 'leftToRight' or 'rightToLeft'. Pass empty string to clear.

legibilityWeight.description: Legibility weight: 'regular' or 'bold'. Pass empty string to clear.

locale.description: BCP 47 locale identifier (e.g., 'en', 'ar', 'ja-JP'). Pass empty string to clear.

name: preview_configure

description: Switch which #Preview block is rendered in a running session. Triggers recompile; @State is reset. Traits persist across switches.

previewIndex.description: 0-based index of the #Preview block to switch to

name: preview_switch

description: Get the accessibility tree of an iOS simulator preview. Returns elements with labels, frames, and traits for targeted interaction.

filter.description: Filter mode: 'all' (default) returns the full tree, 'interactable' returns only buttons/links/toggles, 'labeled' returns only elements with label/value/identifier

sessionID.description: Session ID from preview_start (iOS simulator only)

name: preview_elements

description: Send a touch event to an iOS simulator preview. Coordinates are in device points. For swipe, x/y is the start point.

sessionID.description: Session ID from preview_start (iOS simulator only)

x.description: X coordinate in points (start point for swipe)

y.description: Y coordinate in points (start point for swipe)

name: preview_touch

description: Capture screenshots under multiple trait configurations in a single call. Renders each variant, snapshots it, then restores original traits. Accepts preset names or JSON trait objects.

quality.description: JPEG quality 0.0-1.0 (default: 0.85). Values >= 1.0 produce PNG output.

variants.description: Array of trait variants to snapshot. Example: ["light", "dark", "accessibility3"]

items.description: Preset name ('light', 'dark', 'xSmall'…'accessibility5', 'rtl', 'ltr', 'boldText') or a JSON object string with any combination of colorScheme, dynamicTypeSize, locale, layoutDirection, legibilityWeight, and an optional label.

name: preview_variants

description: List available iOS simulator devices with their UDIDs and states.
name: simulator_list

description: List all active preview sessions in the daemon, with their source file paths and platforms. Used by CLI commands to resolve --file to --session, and for diagnostic tooling.
name: session_list

description: Report the running server's binary path, mtime, and process start time. Returns stale=true when the on-disk binary has been replaced since the running process started — i.e., a swift build happened but the resident MCP server wasn't restarted. Used by the integration-test skill to detect stale-binary footguns before validating behavior.
name: preview_build_info
